body:
## 🐛 Fix: file-creation requests route to `write`, never `read` (#681)

A natural-language **file-creation** request —
`"Create a file named hello.txt with the content hello world"` — emitted a
**`read` tool_call on the not-yet-existing target** instead of a `write`, even
when both `read` and `write` tools were advertised. The CLI then tried to read a
nonexistent file, finished with `File not found`, and wrote nothing.

Fixes #681 · umbrella #680

## 🔎 Root cause

Two independent defects both had to be fixed:

1. **Read intercepts write.** `file_read_task_for` was consulted before the
   general write/create planner, and `has_file_read_intent`
   (`src/agentic_coding/file_read.rs`) matched the keyword **`content`** — which
   appears in *"with the **content** hello world"* — so a write request was
   classified as a read and the read recipe won.
2. **The write planner missed the phrasing.** Even if the write path had been
   reached, `extract_target` did not accept a filename after **`named`**, and
   `extract_content` did not recognise **`with the content`**, so
   `compose_general_change_plan` returned `None` for the issue's exact wording.

## 🛠️ Fix (general, not per-phrase)

- **`has_file_write_intent`** (`general_planner.rs`) — a single routing signal:
  a write verb (`create`/`write`/`save`/`generate`/… across EN + RU) co-occurring
  with a file signal (the word *file*/*файл*, a naming word *named*/*called*/
  *именем*/*названием*, or a filename-looking token). The read recipe now
  **declines up-front** whenever this fires: *write intent beats read intent*.
- **Broadened extraction**, kept as ordered data so a new phrasing is one entry,
  never a code branch:
  - `TARGET_PRECEDING_WORDS` now includes `named`/`called`/`write`/`save`/
    `generate`/… so the target is found after the naming/verb words.
  - `CONTENT_MARKERS` is longest-first so `with the content` wins over a bare
    `content` substring, alongside `with the text`/`that says`/`containing`/… and
    their Russian equivalents.

No literal phrase tables in production paths — the gate recognises the *meaning*
(write-a-file), consistent with the meta-algorithm generalization principle.

## ✅ Tests

**Per-requirement unit tests** + **whole-task E2E** (`tests/unit/issue_681.rs`):

- `file_creation_request_emits_write_not_read` — both tools advertised → `write`.
- `file_creation_never_routes_to_read_even_when_only_read_is_advertised` — never
  a `read` of the file it was asked to create (declining is acceptable).
- `general_change_plan_handles_named_and_with_the_content_phrasing` — the write
  planner extracts `hello.txt` + `hello world`.
- `diverse_write_phrasings_all_route_to_write` — create/write/save/generate, each
  with a distinct filename/content, all route to `write`.
- `file_creation_end_to_end_writes_the_target_and_never_reads` — the full
  agentic loop writes `hello.txt` with its content, never reads, and verification
  observes the written content.

**Real Agent-CLI E2E in CI** (`.github/workflows/release.yml`): a new step drives
the exact reproduction live through `@link-assistant/agent` ↔ `formal-ai serve`
and asserts `hello.txt` is written with `hello world` (write plan → write target
→ verify → final).

Full unit suite: no regressions (the read-recipe regression tests for #627 still
route pure-read prompts to `read`).

## ♻️ Reproduction

```bash
curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/openai/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' -H 'authorization: Bearer formal-ai' \
  -d '{"model":"formal-ai","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Create a file named hello.txt with the content hello world"}],
       "tools":[{"type":"function","function":{"name":"write","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"filePath":{"type":"string"},"content":{"type":"string"}}}}},
                {"type":"function","function":{"name":"read","parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"filePath":{"type":"string"}}}}}]}'
# Before: tool_calls: ["read"]  →  After: tool_calls: ["write"] (hello.txt ← "hello world")
```

## 📚 Data log

The investigation artifacts remain under `dev/log/issues/681/pulls/684/`
(issue/PR/related snapshots + `code/code-references.md` documenting the root cause).

commits.messageBody:
Adding .gitkeep for PR creation (default mode).
This file will be removed when the task is complete.

Issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/681

Compile data related to issue #681 (write→read wrong-tool misroute) and
PR #684 into dev/log/issues/681/pulls/684/, mirroring the structure of
PR #683 for issue #680:

- issue/  : issue #681 JSON + rendered + comments
- pull/   : PR #684 JSON + rendered + diff + comments/reviews
- related/: umbrella #680 and sibling issues 602/604/607/628/671, related PR search
- code/   : root-cause source snapshots (planner/file_read/general_planner/
            change_request/driver) + endpoint-dispatch excerpts + closest tests,
            with code-references.md pinpointing the has_file_read_intent("content")
            misfire and the read-before-write router order.

Data-collection only; no source behavior changed.
commits.messageHeadline: docs(issue-681): compile issue/PR/related data into dev/log
Add development log for issue #681 PR #684

commits.messageBody:
A natural-language file-creation request ("Create a file named hello.txt
with the content hello world") was emitting a `read` tool_call on the
not-yet-existing target instead of a `write`. Two root causes:

1. The file-read recipe was consulted before the write path and matched
   on the word "content" in "...with the content...", classifying a
   write as a read.
2. The general write planner failed to extract the target after "named"
   and the content after "with the content", so it returned no plan and
   the router fell through to read.

Fix, kept general rather than per-phrase:
- Add `has_file_write_intent` (a write verb co-occurring with a file
  signal) as the single routing signal, and gate the read recipe on it:
  write intent beats read intent across every supported language.
- Broaden target extraction (TARGET_PRECEDING_WORDS: named/called/write/
  save/generate/…) and content extraction (CONTENT_MARKERS, longest-first
  so "with the content" wins over bare "content").

All new phrasings are single data entries, never code branches.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(agentic): route file-creation requests to write, never read (#681)

commits.messageBody:
Per-requirement unit tests plus a whole-task E2E through run_agentic_task:
- file-creation emits write not read (both tools advertised)
- never routes to read even when only read is advertised
- the write planner extracts "named …" + "with the content …"
- diverse create/write/save/generate phrasings all route to write
- end-to-end: the target is written with its content and never read,
  and verification observes the written content.

…al Agent CLI

Add a live Agent-CLI ↔ formal-ai E2E step for the exact issue #681
reproduction ("Create a file named hello.txt with the content hello
world"): the real CLI must walk the write/create plan and leave
hello.txt on disk with its content, proving the write-not-read fix
holds end-to-end and not only in the in-process planner tests.
commits.messageHeadline: ci(agentic): drive issue #681 file-creation write case through the re…
docs(changelog): add issue #681 write-not-read fix fragment (patch)

commits.messageBody:
The merge from main brought a newer toolchain whose rustfmt defaults
reformat the const arrays one-per-line and whose clippy flags doc_markdown
(`tool_call`) and a redundant clone in the tests. Apply both so the
lint/format CI gate stays green. No behavior change.
commits.messageHeadline: style(agentic): apply rustfmt and clippy fixes for issue #681 files

title: fix(agentic): route file-creation requests to write, not read (#681)
